growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: Batch cultivation showed that T. versutus AL2T grew much faster than T. nitratis ALJ2 (mumax of 0.064/h versus 0.024/h, respectively) at 10 degrees C ... (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.064 +/- 0.012 /h (Table 1, T. versutus AL2T at 10C, batch). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.064 = 10.83 h. Medium and aeration from Methods (verbatim). pH adjusted to 9.8 at both 10 and 30C. Thiosulfate 40 mM is the electron donor oxidized to sulfate (chemolithoautotroph); no specific uptake/secretion flux (mmol/gDW/h) reported.

up.method: chemolithoautotrophic electron donor; 40 mM in medium, oxidized to sulfate; no specific rate reported
autotrophic carbon source; no specific rate reported

sec.method: end product of complete thiosulfate oxidation; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: this was the opposite at 30 degrees C (mumax of 0.28/h versus 0.43/h) (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.28 +/- 0.064 /h (Table 1, T. versutus AL2T at 30C, batch). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.28 = 2.48 h.

up.method: growth-limiting electron donor (thiosulfate-limited chemostat), 40 mM in feed; no specific uptake flux reported
autotrophic carbon source; no specific rate reported

sec.method: main oxidation end product; no specific rate reported
accumulation observed in chemostats at 10C (not at 30C), indicating slowed second oxidation step at low temperature; not quantified

growth_records.cite: Ahn et al. Molecular and Physiological Adaptations to Low Temperature in Thioalkalivibrio Strains Isolated from Soda Lakes with Different Temperature Regimes. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01202-20. [PMC8092127]
growth_records.snippet: grown at 10 and 30 degrees C in 500-ml chemostat reactors under continuous cultivation with a dilution rate of 0.010 +/- 0.00027/h ... accumulation of elemental sulfur was observed in the chemostats ... at low temperatures
growth_records.notes: At chemostat steady state mu = dilution rate D = 0.010 +/- 0.00027 /h (Methods). doubling_time = ln2/D = 0.6931/0.010 = 69.31 h. growth_method=calculated (mu=D). Thiosulfate-limited; complete oxidation of thiosulfate to sulfate, but elemental sulfur accumulated at 10C (second oxidation step slowed). No specific fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) or yields reported numerically.
